title: Skill Search Engine

classes.name: SkillIndex
classes.description: Skill index entry aligned with server schema
methods: from_dict(d: dict) -> SkillIndex  [classmethod, filters known fields via __dataclass_fields__]

classes.name: SearchResult
classes.description: Single retrieval result wrapping a SkillIndex with scoring metadata

classes.name: SkillSearchError
classes.description: Custom exception raised on API or network errors

classes.name: CLIFormatter
classes.description: CLI entry point and formatting utilities
methods: format_results(results: list[SearchResult], env: dict, query: str) -> str

environment_detection: _detect_tools() -> list[str]  [shutil.which checks 14 CLI tools incl. git/docker/kubectl/terraform]
detect_environment() -> dict  [aggregates os/shell/runtimes/tools + model capability dict]
api_layer: _api_request(endpoint: str, payload: dict) -> dict  [POST JSON via urllib, Bearer auth, timeout=30s, raises SkillSearchError]
public_api: search(query: str, env: dict | None, category: str | None, top_k: int=10) -> list[SearchResult]  [calls _api_request('search', ...) -> SearchResult.from_dict each result]

relationships: search() calls detect_environment() [if env is None]
search() calls SearchResult.from_dict() for each result
get_stats() calls detect_environment() [if env is None]
CLIFormatter(format_results_json) reads SearchResult and SkillIndex attributes
__init__.py re-exports: SkillIndex, SearchResult, SkillSearchError, search, get_stats, detect_environment
key_design_notes: No class named 'SkillSearchEngine' exists — search engine behaviour is implemented via module-level functions (search, get_stats) in engine.py
SkillQuery is not a class — query parameters are plain function arguments (query: str, env: dict, category: str, top_k: int)
SkillResult maps conceptually to SearchResult dataclass
default_factory=list is used on all list fields in both SkillIndex and SearchResult to avoid mutable default issues
API transport uses stdlib urllib only (no requests dependency)
Environment detection is automatic and injected into every API call payload
Quality scoring: clarity*0.3 + completeness*0.3 + actionability*0.4 (SkillIndex.quality_score property)
Public package surface defined in __init__.py: 6 exported symbols
